h2h_summary: Mexico (most likely Winner Group A) hold a narrow all-time edge over Scotland (most likely 3rd-place qualifier from Group C) with only one confirmed international meeting — a 1-0 Mexico win in a 2018 friendly at Azteca — making this a historically thin rivalry with limited data.

notable_results: 1-0 Mexico - International Friendly - 2018 (Giovani dos Santos, Azteca)

key_players_home: Santiago Gimenez (centre-forward, AC Milan, Mexico's sharpest scorer at 24)
Guillermo Ochoa (goalkeeper, record sixth World Cup appearance)
Cesar Huerta (winger, Anderlecht, pace and directness on the flanks)
key_players_away: Scott McTominay (attacking midfielder, Napoli, Scotland's talisman and top scorer in qualifying)
Andy Robertson (left back, captain, 92 caps, Liverpool)
John McGinn (central midfielder, Aston Villa, 85 caps, Europa League winner)
Che Adams (centre-forward, main striker leading the line)
Lawrence Shankland (striker, Hearts, clinical finisher and alternative to Adams)
team_style_home: Mexico under Javier Aguirre deploy a compact 4-3-3 with Edson Alvarez as single pivot, emphasising quick passing through midfield, wing speed via Huerta and Alvarado, and tactical flexibility to shift into a 5-3-2 or 4-3-2 when defending.
team_style_away: Scotland under Steve Clarke set up in a pragmatic 4-3-3 or 3-5-2 built on defensive organisation and a compact mid-block, looking to win the ball quickly and transition via McTominay's late runs into the box and Robertson's overlapping from left back.
wc_history_home: Mexico have appeared at 18 World Cups, are co-hosting the 2026 edition for a record third time (previously 1970 and 1986), and have twice reached the quarter-finals as hosts but are historically plagued by seven consecutive last-16 exits from 1994 to 2018.
wc_history_away: Scotland qualified for their first World Cup since 1998, having appeared eight times in total but never advancing beyond the group stage — their most famous moment remains a 3-2 win over the Netherlands in the 1978 tournament in Argentina.
group_context: Group A (Mexico, South Korea, Czechia, South Africa) has Mexico as heavy co-host favourites to top the group and advance to play the best 3rd-place team from Groups C/E/F/H/I at Azteca; Scotland (Group C: Brazil, Morocco, Haiti) is the most widely predicted best-3rd qualifier from this pool, finishing third behind Brazil and Morocco.
strengths_home: Massive co-host home advantage at Estadio Azteca with 87,000-capacity crowd support
Dual striking threat with Santiago Gimenez's finishing and Raul Jimenez's physical presence
CONCACAF Nations League and Gold Cup titles in 2024-25 reflect strong recent tournament form under Aguirre
strengths_away: McTominay's ability to arrive late into the box from deep midfield creates unpredictable goal threat
Defensively disciplined and hard to break down — Steve Clarke's system concedes very few goals
Experienced core (Robertson 92 caps, McGinn 85 caps) battle-hardened in European club football
